text: Increasing the longitudinal bending stiffness of running shoes within an optimal range can benefit performance-related variables such as running economy.
Softer midsole materials in running shoes reduce impact forces and loading rates during running.
Thicker midsoles provide better cushioning and shock attenuation during impact but may decrease plantar sensory feedback.
Minimalist shoes can improve running economy and increase the cross-sectional area and stiffness of the Achilles tendon compared to conventional shoes.
Minimalist shoes increase metatarsophalangeal and ankle joint loading compared to conventional running shoes, raising injury risk at those sites.
Evidence on the biomechanical effects of heel-toe drop, heel flare, heel cup, shoe lacing, and upper construction remains insufficient to form definitive guidelines.
